examples: A hundred and fifty years ago, a plainsman could kill an American bison, cut out only the tongue for his dinner.
A hundred and fifty years ago a plainsman could kill an American bison, cut out only the tongue for his dinner, and discard the rest of the animal.
Rather than being dissed as a taciturn intellectual, he can be respected as reticent, self-controlled Plainsman, a Gary Cooper, if you will.
